subject: PhD Algebra
instructions: Answer seven problems. Write your answers clearly in complete English sentences. You may quote results (within reason) as long as you state them clearly.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Give the definition of a free object in a concrete category (mathcal C).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Let (mathcal G) be the category of finite groups. Prove that an object (H in mathcal G) is free if and only if (H) is a trivial group.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define what it means for a group (G) to be solvable.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Let (G) be a solvable group and let (N) be a normal subgroup of (G). Prove that (G/N) is solvable.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Let (F) be a group which is free on (n>1) generators. Prove that (F) is not solvable.

problems.text:
Let (m,n) be positive integers. Find (r) such that there is an isomorphism of (mathbb Z)-modules
[
(mathbb Z/mmathbb Z)otimes_{mathbb Z}(mathbb Z/nmathbb Z)cong mathbb Z/rmathbb Z.
]
Justify your answer.
Let (R) be a ring. Prove that the following are equivalent:

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Every short exact sequence of (R)-modules splits.

problems.text: Let (G) be a group, let (F) be a field, and let (chi_1,ldots,chi_n) be distinct homomorphisms from (G) into (F^times). Prove that (chi_1,ldots,chi_n) are linearly independent over (F).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that (Phi_n(X)) is irreducible over (mathbb Q). You may assume that (Phi_n(X)inmathbb Z[X]).

problems.text: Let (R) be a commutative Noetherian ring with (1).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define what it means for a proper ideal (Jsubset R) to be irreducible.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that every irreducible proper ideal in (R) is primary.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Let (Isubset R) be an ideal. Prove that (I) has a primary decomposition.

problems.text: Let (S) be a commutative ring with (1), let (R) be a subring of (S) which contains (1), and let (xin S). Suppose there is a subring (Tsubset S) such that (Tsupset R[x]) and (T) is finitely generated as an (R)-module. Prove that (x) is a root of some monic polynomial with coefficients in (R).
Let (R) be a Noetherian integral domain with a unique nonzero prime ideal (P). Assume that (R) is integrally closed in its fraction field (F). Prove that (P) is a principal ideal.
Determine which of the following are Dedekind domains. Give a brief explanation in each case.
Let (D) be a finite division ring. Prove that (D) is a field.
